cscBeginnerGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Beginner Guide
cscBeginnerGuide.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner rewards players who understand the order of operations before diving into each job. Learn the cleanup loop, tool choices, and early habits that save time across every mission.

steps.title: Scan the Whole Scene First
steps.description: Before touching anything, walk through the full location. Each mission in Crime Scene Cleaner has a different mess layout, and scanning the scene first helps you spot hidden blood trails, evidence, and blockers before you start cleaning.

steps.title: Clear Bodies and Trash Early
steps.description: Body disposal and trash pickup open your movement lines and reveal hidden stains and evidence underneath. Doing this first reduces backtracking and helps you find items you would otherwise miss.

steps.title: Use the Right Tool for Each Surface
steps.description: The mop handles general bloodstains and open floor areas. The sponge is better for delicate objects, carpets, and furniture. The pressure washer works well on large surfaces but can cause extra mess if used carelessly near fragile setups.

steps.title: Manage Your Bucket
steps.description: Both the mop and sponge get dirty over time. Rinse them before they start spreading contamination instead of removing it. Fresh water resets your tool, but it also removes detergent buffs, so time your rinses carefully.

steps.title: Do a Full Sweep Before Leaving
steps.description: Completion in Crime Scene Cleaner is tracked per area. Before finishing a job, do a final walkthrough of every room including ceilings, high shelves, and furniture undersides. Hidden stains and missed items are the most common reason for lower scores.

steps.title: Invest in Core Upgrades First
steps.description: Upgrade your mop resistance, bucket capacity, and cleaning speed before spending on niche tools. These core improvements pay off across every mission and reduce friction on larger, more complex maps.

quickTips: Completion percentage is tracked per area, not per mission overall.
A dirty mop eventually spreads stains instead of removing them.
Check ceilings, high fixtures, and furniture surfaces — blood splatter appears everywhere.
Bucket rinsing removes detergent effects, so plan your bucket use before rinsing.

cscWalkthrough.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Walkthrough
cscWalkthrough.intro: Each Crime Scene Cleaner mission has its own layout, cleanup priorities, and secrets to find. Use this hub to understand what to expect from key missions and how to approach them efficiently.

cards.name: Bad Call
cards.description: The first main mission and the clearest introduction to the full cleanup loop. Focus on learning full-clear habits, check the attic and backroom carefully, and use this mission to build the foundations for larger maps.

cards.name: Trial by Blood
cards.description: Set in Miko's Spa and Wellness, this mission tests how well you switch between broad cleaning and careful detail work in a more layered, decorative interior. Track secrets and cassette pickups while the rooms are still fresh.

cards.name: Affair with Death
cards.description: The Mountainside Villa is one of the larger early jobs with five bodies and plenty of evidence. Route floor by floor instead of improvising and check locked side rooms as part of your main circuit.

cards.name: Amber Alert
cards.description: An Act 2 completion-heavy mission with collectibles, secrets, and multiple cleanup goals. Approach it like a 100 percent clear mission and track collectibles alongside main objectives.

milestones: Learn the cleanup loop in Bad Call
Clear Mountainside Villa with a floor-by-floor route
Reach full completion in Amber Alert

cscUpgradesGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Upgrades Guide
cscUpgradesGuide.intro: The best upgrades in Crime Scene Cleaner are the ones that reduce friction on every single mission. Start with core tool improvements before spending on niche paths that only matter in specific situations.

items.name: Basic Mop Upgrades
items.description: The mop is your main all-purpose tool and affects blood cleanup on nearly every map. Improving mop performance reduces dirty-tool downtime and is the strongest early investment.

items.name: Bucket Upgrades
items.description: Bucket management is central because both mops and sponges rely on rinsing. Better bucket capacity and performance reduces resets and supports detergent use.

items.name: Sponge Upgrades
items.description: The sponge handles fragile objects, carpets, and furniture surfaces. It becomes more valuable as mission variety increases and you encounter more complex indoor layouts.

items.name: Additional Bucket
items.description: The base of the two-bucket strategy. Run one bucket with detergent for cleaning buffs and one with plain water for rinsing, which helps you hold detergent effects much longer.

items.name: Cleaning Speed
items.description: Speed upgrades affect the pace of nearly every mission and reduce time loss on larger jobs. Among the most recommended early skill picks across community guides.

items.name: Pressure Washer Path
items.description: Excellent for large open surfaces but more situational than mop and sponge upgrades. Best used in areas with low risk of creating extra mess from overspray.

items.name: Ozonator Utility
items.description: Weakens nearby stains and softens rooms while you clean elsewhere. A useful support tool on larger jobs, but not a first-buy priority compared to core cleaning improvements.

items.name: Detergent Investment
items.description: Detergents are strong but can be delayed if you use the two-bucket setup. Focus on raw cleaning tool efficiency first and add detergent depth once your core loop is stable.

cscMopSkills.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Mop Skills Guide
cscMopSkills.intro: The mop is the backbone of almost every cleanup run in Crime Scene Cleaner. A strong mop setup reduces rinsing interruptions, widens each cleaning pass, and keeps your loop efficient across long missions.

solutions.name: Increased Resistance
solutions.description: One of the standout mop skills because it extends how long the mop stays effective before getting too dirty to clean. Directly reduces downtime caused by forced rinsing.

solutions.name: Increased Range
solutions.description: Each cleaning pass covers more floor area, which adds up quickly in open-plan interiors with repeated stains across large surfaces.

solutions.name: Cleaning Speed
solutions.description: Frequently recommended because it improves the pace of routine stain removal across nearly every mission. Pairs well with players going for faster full clears.

solutions.name: Clean and Power Detergents
solutions.description: Detergent support improves stain handling and helps the mop stay effective for longer. Use with a dedicated detergent bucket to hold cleaning buffs through long sessions.

solutions.name: Careful Steps
solutions.description: A practical pick for players minimizing accidental rework, especially in cluttered indoor areas where pathing mistakes cost extra cleaning time.

solutions.name: Two-Bucket Setup
solutions.description: One bucket with detergent for buffs and one with plain water for rinsing. The most widely shared mop-adjacent optimization for maintaining a stronger cleaning loop over time.

managementTips: A dirty mop eventually creates new stains instead of removing them — rinse before this threshold.
Use one detergent bucket and one water bucket to hold cleaning buffs without losing them on every rinse.
Increased Range is especially valuable in open floor areas where coverage per pass matters most.
Build the mop setup first before committing to pressure washer or ozonator paths.

cscMoneyGuide.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Money Guide
cscMoneyGuide.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner rewards players who treat each map like both a cleanup contract and a scavenger route. The strongest money runs come from fully exploring the scene, looting valuables, and detouring into secret areas that hide safes, rare items, or large cash stashes.

items.title: Fully clear the scene before leaving
items.description: Clean blood, remove trash, collect evidence, and reset misplaced objects. Perfect-score runs are tied to better mission completion value and long-term progression.
items.tip: Multiple perfect-score achievements require cleaning everything, collecting every piece of trash, restoring objects, and finding all clues.

items.title: Use Crime Sense and clue checks often
items.description: Sweep rooms for missed stains, hidden evidence, and easy-to-overlook interaction points before you exit. Missed evidence or stains can cost score, slowing efficient progression.
items.tip: Bad Call perfect-score guidance breaks scoring into separate sections — a single missed category can ruin the run.

items.title: Steal valuables whenever the route is safe
items.description: Pick up cash, valuables, and special loot during missions instead of treating them as optional flavor items. There is a global achievement for stealing $50,000, confirming that theft is a core income path.
items.tip: The global achievement 'Money, Money, Money' tracks total stolen cash across all runs.

items.title: Prioritize secret rooms with loot
items.description: Take side paths that lead to hidden rooms, safes, and concealed stash areas. Several secrets directly lead to money-related rewards or valuable pickups worth revisiting.
items.tip: Italian Job hides the gambling room and safe for the 'Treasure' achievement, while Affair With Death hides a basement cash-paper stash for 'Money For Nothing'.

items.title: Grab high-value one-off pickups during achievement routes
items.description: Fold collectible or secret-objective items into normal clears instead of replaying later. You can combine money progress with achievement progress in the same mission run.
items.tip: Bad Call contains the miraculous fertilizer for 'Strawberry Fields Forever', and several secret routes also contain tapes or access keys that make a run more efficient overall.

items.title: Explore first, then clean in a loop
items.description: Do a fast reconnaissance pass before deep cleaning so you understand where bodies, clues, locked rooms, and secret paths connect. Exploration reduces backtracking and helps collect loot in one pass.
items.tip: The most efficient maps reward players who know secret entrances, rooftop routes, hidden passages, and locker or safe chains before committing to a final cleanup.

cscPerfectScore.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Perfect Score Guide
cscPerfectScore.intro: Perfect-score runs are built on discipline, not speed. The game repeatedly expects total cleanup, full clue collection, and proper object restoration before you leave a mission.

steps.title: Start with a scouting lap
steps.description: Before deep cleaning, walk the whole scene and identify bodies, key rooms, clue hotspots, secret doors, and any locked side areas. A fast reconnaissance pass prevents late backtracking and reduces missed hidden objectives.

steps.title: Clear the three scoring pillars first
steps.description: Treat cleaning, evidence, and body handling as separate score buckets. Do not assume one perfect category will carry the others — Bad Call's perfect-score guidance explicitly frames missions around separate scoring sections.

steps.title: Remove every visible stain and piece of trash
steps.description: Do a room-by-room sweep instead of random spot cleaning. Check corners, side rooms, bathrooms, and routes behind breakable boards or vents to catch everything the game tracks.

steps.title: Restore objects and fix the scene state
steps.description: Put displaced items back in their original place and avoid creating new mess while you work. Mission-perfect achievement descriptions consistently require putting every object back where it belongs.

steps.title: Find all clues before the final exit
steps.description: Do not rush the last objective trigger. Re-check clue-related rooms, hidden passages, and extra compartments before ending the mission — perfect-score achievements across multiple maps require finding all clues.

steps.title: Clear the map-specific extras
steps.description: Some levels hide score-relevant content in side areas. Italian Job is a major example because perfect cleanup also includes Granny's Apartment. Maps with detached spaces are easy to under-clear on the main path alone.

steps.title: Use easier maps to practice clean execution
steps.description: Toxic Love is widely treated as a forgiving map for cleaner execution and side-achievement routing. Practicing on cleaner layouts helps build habits before harder late-game maps like Modern Art or Call In Dead.

steps.title: Finish with a final verification lap
steps.description: Check bathrooms, vents, hidden cupboards, rooftop paths, and any room unlocked by keys, cards, or switches before you leave. Late misses are usually in optional-looking spaces that still count toward full completion.

cscAchievements.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Achievements Guide
cscAchievements.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner tracks a large achievement set, but completion players usually start with perfect-score milestones, money-related secrets, and map-specific hidden interactions. The fastest route combines mission-perfect goals with secret-path achievements in the same run.

items.name: Bad Call
items.requirement: Complete Bad Call mission with perfect score

items.name: Trial by Blood
items.requirement: Complete Trial by Blood mission with perfect score

items.name: Gold On The Ceiling
items.requirement: Find and use the V.I.P card

items.name: Get Lucky
items.requirement: Find and open the hidden safe

items.name: Treasure
items.requirement: Get to the gambling room and open the safe

items.name: Money For Nothing
items.requirement: Get rid of worthless paper from the basement

items.name: Another Brick in the Wall
items.requirement: Touch the lamp and see what happens

cscSecrets.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Secrets Guide
cscSecrets.intro: Secret hunting matters because it overlaps with money, collectibles, and achievements. Several of the game's best side rewards are hidden behind vents, rooftop paths, fake walls, and key-card chains.

sections.name: Bad Call
sections.summary: 3 tapes and 3 secrets. Strong early chapter for building secret habits — teaches rooftop access, hidden keys, and optional rooms.
details: Break boards at the barred window and carefully cross the planks to reach the rooftop secret.
Pick up the first key in the boarded room, then combine it with the second key route to open the gated bathroom-area secret.
The miraculous fertilizer for Strawberry Fields Forever is tied to this chapter's secret routing.

sections.name: Italian Job
sections.summary: 3 tapes and 3 secrets. One of the best chapters for money-focused secret play.
details: Take the key from the kitchen drawer, open the hatch under the office boxes, then hack the wall safe in the hidden gambling room.
Open the side door, break the wooden planks, and continue through the hidden area for another secret branch.
Use a ladder or stacked boxes to get over the brick wall in the backyard for Rock and Roll All Nite.

sections.name: Affair With Death
sections.summary: 3 tapes and 3 secrets. Excellent chapter for combining secrets with money and side achievements.
details: Use the ladder near the basketball court or circle up the stairs and follow the edge to the tree-side secret.
Crawl through the hole behind the boxes to reach the hidden paper-money room for Money For Nothing.
Collect the toilet key on the first floor and use it on the ground-floor laundry room.

sections.name: Short Circuit
sections.summary: 2 tapes and 2 secrets. Compact map with easy-to-miss interaction secrets.
details: Interact with the tilted wall lamp on the first floor to open the hidden door and unlock Another Brick in the Wall.
Check the little door next to the fridge area for the second secret space.
This is a short chapter and a good replay map for clean secret-routing practice.

sections.name: Modern Art
sections.summary: 4 tapes and 2 secrets. Puzzle-heavy chapter with memorable secret design.
details: Open the glass cases to build a staircase, interact with the golden cassette, and reveal the hidden room beside the stage.
Solving the monitor sequence opens the second secret door in the labyrinth area.
This map contains many breakable objects, making it efficient for stacking secret hunting with destruction-related progress.

sections.name: Call In Dead
sections.summary: 4 tapes and 5 secrets. The densest secret chapter in the base campaign and one of the best replay maps for completionists.
details: Break the vent in the power supply room and crawl through for the first secret.
Climb the stack of boxes on hall ground floor, duck under the metal beam, and follow the hidden path to another secret.
Climb the lockers and pipes, break into the vent, and enter Zack's room for Lookin' Out My Back Door.

cscCassetteTapes.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Cassette Tapes Guide
cscCassetteTapes.subtitle: Track the music collectibles players most often miss on return visits. This hub works best when grouped by mission and paired with short route notes.
cscCassetteTapes.intro: Crime Scene Cleaner hides cassette tapes and discs inside side rooms, puzzle rewards, and easy-to-skip corners. A practical collectibles page should not only name the item, but also tell the player what room it sits in and what action unlocks it.

items.location: Botanical Room behind the iron door in the courtyard
items.access: Find the first key on a box under the balcony, get the second key from the bathroom in the secret corridor, then open the Botanical Room

items.location: Shelf on the floor below the dealer's apartment lift
items.access: Break the boards to reach the balcony, lower the lift, then edge along the wall to the lower floor

items.location: Bathroom in the right-hand side cabin
items.access: Direct pickup once the cabin area is opened

items.location: TV table in the party house
items.access: Direct pickup in the main party house interior

items.location: Next to the blue radio in the Red Room
items.access: Direct pickup while clearing the East Wing

items.location: On the stage in the West Wing cassette room
items.access: Direct pickup in the cassette room

items.location: Duck Room Corridor floor next to the box
items.access: Found after climbing up from the Duck Room into the corridor

items.location: Reward mannequin outside the secret TV room
items.access: Solve the TV-room puzzle, enter the secret room, defeat all enemies with the katana, then collect the reward on exit

cscBodiesEvidence.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Bodies and Evidence Guide
cscBodiesEvidence.subtitle: This page helps players finish runs that stall at 95 to 99 percent. The best version is a mission checklist built around totals and the most commonly missed rooms or objects.
cscBodiesEvidence.intro: The usual failure point in Crime Scene Cleaner is not the main cleanup loop but the final missing interaction: one body in a side corridor, one clue tucked into a victim, or one object that did not register because the player skipped the required interaction.

checkpoints: Bodies are in the Kitchen, Backroom, and Attic
Evidence appears across the Lounge, Bonsai Room, Kitchen, Backroom, Attic #1, and Attic #3
Central Exhibition objectives include 2 evidence and 34 furniture placements
Dark Room has 1 body and a Shield evidence item on top of the victim
Duck Room has 2 bodies plus Katana and Sword evidence items
Duck Room Corridor has 1 body and a Note inside the box
Ground Floor objectives include 5 evidence and 21 furniture placements
First Floor objectives include 6 evidence and 8 furniture placements
Bedroom 1 evidence: Phone on top of the drawer unit next to the bed
Bedroom 2 evidence: Smart Speaker next to the drawer unit by the wardrobe
If a speaker objective seems stuck, stop sweeping visually and follow the sound cue
Some players reported the third speaker can fail to register immediately
Computer interactions can count as objective progress, so re-check erased footage or triggered devices before restarting

cscTrueCleaner.title: Crime Scene Cleaner True Cleaner Guide
cscTrueCleaner.subtitle: True Cleaner punishes sloppy routing more than normal missions do. A good guide should combine universal habits with mission examples that show where hidden dirt usually survives.
cscTrueCleaner.intro: The fastest way to stabilize True Cleaner runs is to separate method from map knowledge. First build a repeatable cleaning routine, then use mission-specific dirt patterns only where the mode tends to waste time: ceilings, door interiors, paw prints, footprints, and rooms that become dirty again.

steps.title: Set up two buckets before the long clean
steps.description: Use one bucket to rinse the mop and another to apply detergent. This is especially useful in True Cleaner mode because detergents are limited.

steps.title: Do a body-and-object sweep before the final polish
steps.description: A proven community method is to leave one body near the truck, clean the rest of the mission first, and avoid ending the mission after accidentally loading that body too early.

steps.title: Follow footprints, paw prints, and handprints instead of scanning randomly
steps.description: On Trial By Blood, dirty footprints lead from the entrance into the Men's Locker Room, and the Relaxation Zone still needs the pool drained and scrubbed. On Short Circuit, the TV Room gets dirtied again by the invisible cat, so follow the pawprints from the foyer door.

steps.title: Check the hidden vertical surfaces every time
steps.description: Players commonly miss dirt on ceilings, around fans, on the inside of doors, behind beds, and around clutter piles. In Trial By Blood, the Sauna dirt spreads across benches, walls, floor, and the inside of the sauna door.

steps.title: Use mission counts to verify the run before exit
steps.description: For example, Bad Call True Cleaner requires 3 bodies, 9 evidence pieces, and 20 furniture restorations. Verify the objective counts room by room before the final truck run.

cscNightmareMode.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Nightmare Mode Guide
cscNightmareMode.subtitle: Nightmare mode remixes familiar maps into more puzzle-like cleanup runs. The most useful page structure is a mission-by-mission accordion that surfaces the special objective, collectible, and room pressure points first.
cscNightmareMode.intro: Nightmare mode is no longer just a harder replay of the same maps. It adds altered layouts, special objectives, and collectible routing that matter for completionist players.

sections.summary: A large multi-wing route with portal navigation, misplaced furniture, evidence cleanup, and an Onyx cassette/disc collectible.
details: Objectives include finding misplaced furniture across the Foyer, Cloud Level, Furniture Exhibition, Cafeteria, Magic Doors, Upper Passage, Music Exhibition, Ancient Exhibition, Sewage Exhibition, and Ducks Exhibition
The mission includes a dedicated cassette/disc objective for the Onyx collectible
Body routing relies on portal landmarks such as the cafeteria, ancient exhibition, furniture exhibition, upper passage, and sewage exhibition

sections.summary: A ritual-focused variant with a separate special objective and a Quartz cassette/disc pickup.
details: This mission adds a special objective: preparing the ritual
The walkthrough also tracks the exact Quartz cassette/disc location
It is best handled as a structured cleanup instead of a freeform sweep

sections.summary: A reworked spa map with more guided evidence and person tracking.
details: Key mission flow includes 7 people to find
The Solarium contains a Dreamcatcher evidence item hanging on the Flamingo statue
The VIP Room contains another Dreamcatcher evidence trigger behind a stone-activated wooden door
Locker cards such as W018 can tie into the Ruby cassette/disc route

sections.section: Why Nightmare Pages Matter After Act 2
sections.summary: Nightmare support keeps expanding alongside new post-launch mission coverage.
details: The game hub added new Act 2 mission walkthroughs in March 2026
Nightmare pages are strongest when they focus on altered objectives, not just higher difficulty wording
Collectors benefit most when the page pairs each nightmare mission with its unique disc or special mechanic

cscAct2Walkthrough.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 Walkthrough
cscAct2Walkthrough.intro: Act 2 adds four new story missions launched on March 26, 2026. Use this mission-by-mission route to clear objectives faster, track hidden content, and avoid backtracking across each new location.

objectives: Clean the main nightclub and back-room areas
Check kitchen, locker-room, and smoke-room puzzle spaces
notable_puzzles: Barrel order puzzle near the hidden elevator area
Dartboard secret route that opens after a precise score sequence
missions.routing_tip: Clear the visible objective spaces first, then sweep the kitchen, locker rooms, and hidden wall sections before final cleanup.

objectives: Search the spa, cabin, and second-floor interior
Check side paths created by breakable roots and hidden tunnels
Handle panel and statue-based secret sequences
notable_puzzles: Hidden path behind roots in the spa area
Second-floor panel combination in the futuristic room
missions.routing_tip: Finish the spa side first, then move upstairs and complete the corridor rooms before returning for statue placement.

objectives: Sweep garage, villa interior, cellar, and terrace
Open hidden passages with painting, duck, and brick triggers
Check first-floor and upper-terrace spaces before exit
notable_puzzles: Black duck destruction sequence for the chained coffin
Hidden brick trigger near the wine-cellar faucet
missions.routing_tip: Do the garage and first-floor clue chain early, then save the cellar and terrace for the end so collectible checks stay organized.

missions.focus: Late Act 2 cleanup with vent and handle secrets
objectives: Open blocked side rooms by clearing trash and obstacles
Check party room, bowling alley, and maintenance room
Use the loose handle to unlock the last hidden area
missions.routing_tip: Treat this mission like a zone-by-zone completion pass: kitchen first, party room second, bowling lanes third, then maintenance room last.

cscAct2Secrets.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Act 2 Secrets and CDs Guide
cscAct2Secrets.intro: Act 2 hidden rooms, collectible CDs, and puzzle triggers across all four new missions. Find each mission's secrets fast with this trigger-and-location reference.

unlock_methods: Arrange the marked barrels in the required order
Use the dartboard sequence so the final dart lands on 1
Place the bust on the empty pedestal
Check the hidden path behind the painting

key_locations: Torch-lit side path near the gate
Second-floor corridor room by the mirror
unlock_methods: Place a ladder and break the roots to access the tunnel
Follow the torch line to the hut area
Search the second-floor room near the mirror
Enter the panel combination in the futuristic room
Place the three monkey statues in the required order
Take the first CD from the garage-side cabinet
Use the three-painting solution to open a hidden route
Destroy all black rubber ducks to open the coffin
Check the terrace greenhouse area for the last CD
Use the key from the niche above the garage entrance
Clean the cobweb and press the hidden brick near the faucet
Clear the blocked door, go outside, and break the grate
Pick up the floor CD in the party room
Take the table CD in the bowling area
Find the loose handle, attach it to the maintenance-room door, and enter the hidden room

cscDetergents.title: Crime Scene Cleaner Detergents Guide
cscDetergents.intro: A practical detergent reference for faster clears, less bucket maintenance, and cleaner mop loops. Learn what each detergent does and when it is worth using.

items.main_effect: Lets tools clean more surfaces before they need rinsing
items.best_for: Long cleaning loops where the mop or sponge gets dirty too quickly
items.usage_note: Strong general-purpose pick for reducing rinse frequency on larger stages

items.main_effect: Makes bucket water last longer by slowing how fast rinsing dirties it
items.best_for: Missions where bucket maintenance is more annoying than stain strength
items.usage_note: Pairs well with a separate plain-water bucket in a two-bucket setup

items.best_for: Hard surfaces and stubborn stains, especially stronger red stains under UV checks
items.usage_note: Useful when speed matters, but also dirties tools and water faster

items.main_effect: Cleans a larger surface area with each pass
items.usage_note: Best when paired with efficient room sweeps rather than precision cleanup

items.main_effect: Hybrid effect combining Resistance and Clean in a weaker form
items.usage_note: Good convenience option for players who want fewer swaps

items.main_effect: Hybrid effect combining Area and Power in a weaker form
items.best_for: Broad blood cleanup where both spread and pressure matter
items.usage_note: Useful when you want one bottle to cover mixed stain situations

items.main_effect: Keep one bucket as detergent water and one as plain rinse water
items.best_for: Long missions and players trying to conserve detergent
items.usage_note: Dip a clean tool into the detergent bucket for the buff, rinse dirty tools in the plain-water bucket, then reapply without constantly remaking the detergent bucket

items.best_for: Planning whether to spend or conserve supplies mid-level
items.usage_note: If you run out during a mission, you finish with what is left; supplies refresh again when a new mission starts

cscSystemInfo.title: Crime Scene Cleaner System Requirements and Updates
cscSystemInfo.intro: PC specs, current review snapshot, and the major content beats that explain why players are returning. Check whether your PC can run the game or whether recent updates make it worth reinstalling.

items.title: Base PC Requirements
details: OS: Windows 10/11
Minimum CPU: i5-7500 or similar
Recommended CPU: i5-10600 or similar
Memory: 16 GB RAM
Minimum GPU: GTX 1060
Recommended GPU: RTX 2070 SUPER
Storage: 35 GB available space
Integrated graphics are not supported
Intel ARC series may not be supported yet

items.title: Review State
details: English reviews: Overwhelmingly Positive with 13,272 reviews
Total reviews in all languages: 28,233
Recent reviews in all languages: 838 and Overwhelmingly Positive

items.title: Challenge Modes Added
details: Added Into the Nightmare mode
Added True Cleaner's Mode with no hints and no hand-holding
Added unlockable skins for tools and cleaning gear

items.title: Community Side Maps
details: Added Alter Ego
Added Development Hell
Expanded side-content variety outside the main story path

items.title: Act 2 Story Expansion
details: Act 2 launched on March 26, 2026
Added the next major story chapter for Kovalsky
Created fresh demand for new walkthroughs, secret routes, and collectible guides

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Crime Scene Cleaner

questions.question: What platforms support Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner is available on Steam for PC, Xbox, and PlayStation 5. All versions include the full campaign with Act 1 and Act 2 content.

questions.question: How many achievements does Crime Scene Cleaner have?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner has 80 Steam achievements covering mission completion, secrets, collectibles, tool upgrades, and difficulty challenges.

questions.question: Does Crime Scene Cleaner have Nightmare Mode?
questions.answer: Yes. Crime Scene Cleaner includes a Nightmare Mode that adds harder cleanup requirements and stricter scoring to existing missions.

questions.question: What is the best mop upgrade in Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: Increased Resistance and Cleaning Speed are the most recommended mop upgrades in Crime Scene Cleaner. They reduce downtime from dirty tools and improve overall cleanup pace.

questions.question: How do I get a perfect score in Crime Scene Cleaner?
questions.answer: A perfect score in Crime Scene Cleaner requires cleaning all blood and stains, disposing of all bodies, collecting all evidence, and restoring displaced furniture.

questions.question: How many copies has Crime Scene Cleaner sold?
questions.answer: Crime Scene Cleaner has sold over 1 million copies. The game launched on Steam in August 2024 and has maintained a very positive rating from over 13,000 English reviews.
